sanskrit:
पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम्।
व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता॥
transliteration:
paśyaitāṃ pāṇḍuputrāṇāmācārya mahatīṃ camūm |
vyūḍhāṃ drupadaputreṇa tava śiṣyeṇa dhīmatā ||
literal_meaning: Behold, O teacher, this great army of the sons of Pāṇḍu, arrayed by your wise disciple, the son of Drupada.
contextual_meaning: Duryodhana’s taunt exposes the painful irony that the very skills Droṇa taught his ‘enemy’ student are now poised to destroy him, mirroring how our own nurtured talents or relationships can turn against us when loyalty splits. Psychologically it is the moment we realize that what we trusted—our mentor, our creation, our self-image—has become the instrument of our downfall, evoking dread of self-betrayal and existential helplessness.
psychological_state: A leader who sees their former protégé excelling on the opposing side and feels the ground of loyalty and competence crumble beneath them.
trigger_scenario: A manager watching their star employee—whom they trained—lead a rival team that will soon decide the manager’s redundancy.
anchor_text: What you create can turn and break you.
